title: Mir Family Config Schema (design artifact)
description: Design-time schema for cross-family enforcement configuration. Defines enabled_phases and hooks_enabled fields referenced by applications/template-cherrypick.md §4-1 and applications/exceptions.md §5. R7-D-W5  —  schema  design-only artifact .  `config/repos/<family>.json` (`config/repo-agent-management.schema.json` )     schema      .  schema     .

phase.description: Family rollout phase number 0-12 from docs/harness-engineering/phase-N-*.md. phase-13 is excluded here because it is the R30 mir-self/template applied-state closure lane, not a per-family enabled_phases target.

strictness.description: Per-phase strictness (R4 4-tier vocabulary, see applications/exceptions.md §3). 'doc-strict' is Phase 0 only.

enabled_at.description: ISO-8601 date when this phase was enabled for this family.

enabled_by.description: User identifier who enabled this phase.

revert_window.description: Duration (e.g., '7d', '2w') during which user can revert this phase without ADR. See exceptions.md §6.

rationale.description: Why this phase was enabled at this strictness for this family.

enabled_phases.description: Per-phase opt-in registry. Empty array means no Mir enforcement applies (family runs free).

status.description: Hook activation status. 'advisory' = log only, no block.

hooks_enabled.description: Per-hook activation map (e.g., {'pre-tool-use': {'status': 'enabled'}, 'tdd-guard': {'status': 'advisory'}}). Hook names are the basenames in `.claude/hooks/`.

self_stop_acknowledged.description: Family operator's acknowledgement that SE-meta self-stop (Mir not-done phase cannot be enforced on family) was checked. Required true before enabled_phases contains any enforced phase.

purpose.description: Single-line mission/purpose statement (R10-R1 , mirror of repo-agent-management.schema.json $defs/repository.purpose). family-private skill recommendation  driver.

character.description: Distinguishing traits — controlled vocabulary (see the repo-agent-management vocab list).
